raw_consumer_statement:
I was involved in a fraud scam. The fraudster used my American Express account to purchase items totaling {$10000.00}. I never gave my card or card number to the fraudster. They represented themselves as being from American Express and were checking on fraudulent purchases that they made. They were very professional. I then got a letter from American Express, saying they recognized the suspicious activity. They proceeded to conduct an investigation. I received a letter ( XXXX ) saying the investigation was completed and charges would be credited to my account. The credits were made, but then reversed at a later date. When I questioned why the charges were now my responsibility, they said I gave the merchant my information willfully ( which I did not ) and they were holding me responsible. ( attached letter XX/XX/23. 

I have included all correspondence between AMEX and me. The correspondence describes the sequence of events regarding the fraud and my communications with AMEX. I have never done business with the merchants involved and AMEX has not given me proof that the merchant verified my identity at the time of purchases
